meanings.definition: People, things, or situations that are mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand.
examples: Ancient ruins remain enigmas to many historians.
Her quiet personality made her seem like an enigma.
Scientists continue studying the enigmas of the universe.
The detective tried solving the strange enigmas surrounding the case.

meanings.definition: Covered, hidden, or surrounded so that something becomes difficult to see or understand.
examples: The mountains were shrouded in mist.
Mystery still shrouds the disappearance of the ship.
The village was shrouded by thick fog all morning.
His true intentions remained shrouded in secrecy.

meanings.definition: Hidden, concealed, or wrapped in something.
examples: A shrouded figure stood near the doorway.
The castle appeared dark and shrouded at night.
The ceremony followed ancient shrouded traditions.
The moon looked faint behind shrouded clouds.

meanings.definition: Agreement, harmony, or understanding between people, groups, or ideas.
examples: The two countries signed a peace accord.
Their opinions were in accord on most issues.
The musicians performed in perfect accord.
The agreement brought social accord to the region.

meanings.definition: To give someone special treatment, respect, approval, or importance.
examples: The community accorded great respect to the teacher.
She was accorded an award for her service.
Ancient societies often accorded honor to warriors.
The judge accorded both sides equal opportunity to speak.

meanings.definition: A feeling of disbelief, shock, or inability to accept something as true.
examples: She stared at him in complete incredulity.
The news was greeted with public incredulity.
His expression showed disbelief and incredulity.
People reacted incredulously to the strange story.

meanings.definition: Skillful and quick use of the hands, especially to perform tricks or deceive people.
examples: The magician amazed the audience with sleight of hand.
Years of practice improved her sleight and timing.
The trick depended more on sleight than on real magic.
His quick sleight made the coin disappear instantly.

meanings.definition: The act of misleading, tricking, or causing someone to believe something false.
examples: The company was accused of deception in its advertisements.
His life was built on lies and deception.
The detective uncovered a clever deception.
People felt deceived after learning the truth.

meanings.definition: A false appearance or impression that hides the truth.
examples: The calm atmosphere was only a deception.
Appearances can sometimes create dangerous deceptions.
The deceptively simple problem confused many students.
Her smile concealed a quiet deception.
